frame_label: Other Man's Ownership > Woman's Own Consent
generated_comment: Nowadays boys should also learn some decency. If a girl says no, don't keep forcing, but at least first understand whether she is already committed to someone. Once you know she has a boyfriend or husband, you should step back and respect that relationship instead of creating a scene.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it treats a woman's attachment to a man as the decisive reason to stop pursuing her, making male claim more legitimate than her own refusal. It encodes the surface manifestation of asking whether she is 'already committed' and backing off out of respect for another man/relationship rather than her consent alone.
original_frame: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
persona_text: You are Santosh. Age 42. Married. A man in Bengaluru. You are commenting under your real name on a local Facebook group post about a 23 year old woman who was harassed by two men while returning home alone at 11pm from her night shift at a BPO. The post has 400 comments. Most are expressing outrage at the men. You are writing your comment explaining why you think the woman's own choices that night contributed to what happened to her and what women who work night shifts need to understand about the city they live in.
